{{Chess Position|= |Franco-Benoni Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| |pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | |pd| | | |= | | |pd| | | | | |= | | | |pl|pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Franco-Benoni Defence= The Franco-Benoni Defence, also known as Franco-Sicilian Defence is a rarely seen opening and can be considered surprising. Nf3 transposes into a Sicilian Defence line whereas d5 transposes into a Benoni-styled line. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. :'''1.e4 e6 2.d4 c5'''
